old_claim.note: Nanotechnology is a plausible dependency for a forecast technology and should be treated as speculative.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because high-temperature superconductors were discovered in 1986 through solid-state oxide materials research, not through nanotechnology as a direct prerequisite.

source_shape.source_locator: Press release describes the 1986 oxide-material discovery and prior superconductivity lineage without nanotechnology as a prerequisite.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: High-Tc superconductivity was discovered in barium-doped lanthanum-copper-oxide ceramic material.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports solid-state materials and chemistry lineage, not a nanotechnology prerequisite.

ontology_before: The graph made an established 1986 material class depend on a broad nanotechnology forecast node.
ontology_after: The graph removes the false nanotechnology prerequisite and uses materials/chemistry/superconductivity lineage instead.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: direct superconductors_high_temp -> nanotechnology edge removed.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to future nanoscale-engineered superconductors rather than established high-Tc materials.
A source showed the 1986 Ba-La-Cu-O discovery depended on nanotechnology as a field.
short_reason: High-Tc superconductors predate and do not require nanotechnology as a direct prerequisite.
